title: PKCS11 put() create-then-destroy ordering prevents data loss
description: pkcs11/src/lib.rs put() collects handles of existing objects BEFORE creating the new one, creates the new object, then destroys old handles. This ordering is correct: if create fails, the existing secret is unmodified (no data loss). If destroy fails after create, put() returns Ok — the write succeeded and the duplicate will be cleaned up on the next put(). A future contributor might suggest 'simplify by destroy-then-create' — that ordering risks data loss if create fails after destroy. The current ordering must be preserved.
